text: The study enrolled 450 patients with a mean age of 65 years.
Blood pressure was reduced by 12 mmHg from baseline.
Adverse events occurred in 23% of patients.
The trial was conducted across 50 clinical sites.
Patients were randomized 1:1 to treatment or placebo.
The primary endpoint was overall survival at 24 months.
Laboratory values remained within normal ranges throughout the study.
The treatment was well tolerated with no dose-limiting toxicities.
Median follow-up was 36 months (range 12-48).
Quality of life scores improved significantly from baseline.
The ratio of responders was approximately 2:1 favoring treatment.
Subgroup analysis showed consistent results across age groups.
The probability of progression at 1 year was 0.35.
Treatment duration ranged from 6 to 24 weeks.
The effect size was moderate according to Cohen's criteria.
Kaplan-Meier curves showed separation at 6 months.
The number needed to treat was calculated as 15.
Interim analysis showed no safety concerns.
Patient characteristics were well balanced between groups.
The study was powered to detect a 25% reduction in events.
